recipe.title: Lasagne dumpling

ingredients.name: lean ground pork
ingredients.note: main raw protein for the farce

ingredients.name: shiitakes
ingredients.note: finely chopped; visible dark flecks in the farce

ingredients.name: green onions
ingredients.note: chopped in farce, plus sliced garnish

ingredients.name: soy sauce
ingredients.note: salty gloss in farce and final finish

ingredients.name: dumpling wrappers
ingredients.note: thin wrappers layered like lasagna sheets

steps.text: Mix ground pork, finely chopped shiitakes, green onions, ginger, garlic, soy sauce, hoisin, rice vinegar, sambal oelek, and toasted sesame oil into a sticky aromatic farce.
steps.visualCue: glossy beige-pink pork paste with dark mushroom flecks, green onion bits, and red sambal streaks
steps.textureCue: raw mince changes from loose crumbles to cohesive sticky paste
steps.runwayRisk: Farce can look dry or like cooked taco meat if the wet glossy bind is not emphasized.

steps.text: Oil an 8 inch / 20 cm square mold.
steps.visualCue: thin clear oil film catching highlights in a compact square baking dish
steps.runwayRisk: Mold scale can drift into a full-size lasagna tray unless locked to 20 cm square.

steps.text: Alternate thin layers of farce and dumpling wrappers, finishing with dumpling wrappers on top.
steps.visualCue: thin farce spread edge-to-edge, pale wrappers tiled or overlapped across the square mold
steps.textureCue: sticky spread under soft floppy wrapper sheets
steps.runwayRisk: Seedance may invent individual folded dumplings, tomato lasagna noodles, cheese, or thick meat patties.

steps.text: Add 3 tbsp water to help steam and hydrate the wrapper stack.
steps.visualCue: small clear splash around the wrapper edges, not a flooded mold
steps.textureCue: water beads slipping into the wrapper seams
steps.runwayRisk: Too much water can read as soup; too little water makes wrappers look dry and uncooked.

steps.text: Steam the mold in a large pot with hot water halfway up the mold, covered, for 25 minutes.
steps.visualCue: covered pot, steam pressure, condensation, then a tender opaque/translucent top layer
steps.textureCue: raw pork sets firm and wrappers turn supple, glossy, and slightly translucent
steps.runwayRisk: The pot setup can be misread as boiling the lasagna directly; water must stay outside the mold except the measured 3 tbsp.

steps.text: Finish with soy sauce, sliced green onion, and white sesame seeds.
steps.visualCue: dark soy gloss streaking over pale steamed wrapper top, green scallion confetti, white sesame specks
steps.textureCue: wet shine, steam, tender stacked cross-section
steps.runwayRisk: Garnish can become sparse dust or oversized confetti without amount and scale locks.

subRecipeDependencies.orderConstraint: Must be fully mixed before layering so the seasoning and bind are evenly distributed.
Must happen before the first farce layer; the oil film helps release the steamed slab.
Thin farce layers must alternate with wrappers and finish with wrappers on top; farce should not be mounded thickly.
The stack must be hydrated with 3 tbsp water, then cooked covered for 25 minutes so raw pork sets safely and wrappers soften.
Garnish comes only after steaming, when the top is tender, glossy, and ready to catch soy sauce.

assumptions: Use a compact 8 inch / 20 cm square mold, not a family-size rectangular lasagna pan.
Treat the approximately 20 dumpling wrappers as palm-size thin wrappers that tile or overlap to cover the square mold.
For visual continuity, cut or lift the final dish as 4 generous square portions unless the creator specifies a different serving style.
Use existing kitchen, character, and utensil assets wherever possible; do not create raw or mid-cooking recipe-state references unless later storyboard testing proves the geometry fails.

criticalTransformations.transformation: Loose pork mince plus wet seasonings becomes a sticky, glossy, spreadable farce.
criticalTransformations.whyFragile: If it looks dry, the later layers read as crumbly meat rather than juicy dumpling filling.
criticalTransformations.visualEvidence: Cohesive paste clings to spoon or spatula, with mushroom flecks and chili streaks.

criticalTransformations.transformation: Pale raw dumpling wrappers become tender, slightly translucent, glossy sheets.
criticalTransformations.whyFragile: Wrappers may be mistaken for lasagna noodles, tortillas, ravioli, or folded dumplings.
criticalTransformations.visualEvidence: Soft sheet surface, visible overlap seams, pale sheen, no browned or crisp edges.
criticalTransformations.referenceNeed: Use text scale locks first; consider a single final top/cut reference only if Seedance misreads the dish.

criticalTransformations.transformation: Raw pink pork farce sets into juicy, cohesive cooked layers.
criticalTransformations.whyFragile: The dish is steamed, so there is no browning; cooked pork must look safe and moist, not raw pink or grey-dry.
criticalTransformations.visualEvidence: Opaque pale beige pork layer, small juices, shiitake flecks, compact slice edge.
criticalTransformations.referenceNeed: A final cutaway reference can help if the storyboard includes a lifted portion.

criticalTransformations.transformation: Covered pot opens to condensation, steam, and a glossy dumpling-lasagna top.
criticalTransformations.whyFragile: Steam can obscure geometry or make the mold look submerged.
criticalTransformations.visualEvidence: Visible square mold rim, water outside the mold, top layer intact and glossy.
criticalTransformations.referenceNeed: No generated state reference needed if the shot stays simple.

criticalTransformations.transformation: Plain pale steamed top becomes high-contrast glossy hero surface.
criticalTransformations.whyFragile: Garnish scale and sauce amount can drift; too much soy makes the dish muddy, too little loses the food-porn payoff.
criticalTransformations.visualEvidence: Thin dark soy ribbons, 2-3 scallion pinches, light sesame scatter, visible steam.

visualTextureOpportunities: Macro soy sauce ribbon sliding across the steamed wrapper top, catching sesame oil shine.
Sticky pork-shiitake farce slapped and spread thin in the square mold.
Dumpling wrappers laid down as floppy overlapping tiles, with soft edge ripples.
Measured 3 tbsp water bead slipping into wrapper seams before steaming.
Lid lift with condensation drip and steam bloom around the compact square mold.
Clean spoon or spatula lift showing soft stacked layers and juicy pork filling.
Scallion and white sesame rain over a glossy pale-and-dark surface.

possibleHooks.description: Start on a tight macro of dark soy sauce glossing a steamy pale dumpling-wrapper top, then immediately clarify the square layered dish.
possibleHooks.reason: Texture-first, sexy, and not a neutral ingredient lineup.

possibleHooks.description: Start on a close lift of one tender layered corner pulling away with steam and juices, then cut back to the fast layering setup.
possibleHooks.reason: Strong food-porn payoff, but should be used carefully because final cutting/lifting as the very first image can feel like a final-dish reveal.

possibleHooks.description: Open on glossy pork-shiitake farce being spread razor-thin, followed by wrappers landing like silk sheets.
possibleHooks.reason: Keeps the opening process-driven while still tactile and appetizing.

scaleGeometryRisks.risk: Dish becomes a normal Western lasagna.
scaleGeometryRisks.guardrail: No tomato sauce, no cheese pull, no long lasagna noodles; use pale dumpling wrappers and pork-shiitake filling only.
Keep wrappers flat, tiled, and layered in one 20 cm square mold; no pleating, sealing, or individual parcels.
8 inch / 20 cm square mold, roughly 4 portions; not a deep catering tray, not miniature ramekins.
Each farce layer should be thin, about 3-5 mm, spread edge-to-edge; not burger-patty slabs.
Approximately 20 palm-size wrappers total, overlapping/tiled; visible seams are acceptable and desirable.

scaleGeometryRisks.risk: Steam bath reads as boiling soup.
scaleGeometryRisks.guardrail: Water halfway up outside the mold, covered pot; only 3 tbsp water inside the mold.
Lift one square portion around 9-10 cm wide from the 20 cm mold, showing 3-5 cm total height and horizontal layers.
Use a light sesame scatter and 2-3 visible pinches of thin scallion, not giant onion pieces or invisible dust.

recipeSpecificReferences.name: FinishedDumplingLasagnaCutaway
recipeSpecificReferences.role: only generated reference recommended if layer geometry must be locked for a lifted/cut final portion
recipeSpecificReferences.why: The non-standard identity is the finished square steamed dumpling-lasagna slab with visible wrapper/farce layers; text alone may drift toward folded dumplings or Western lasagna.
recipeSpecificReferences.avoidIf: Avoid creating this if the storyboard can communicate the dish with simple top, layering, steam, and garnish shots.

notRecommended: No intermediate steaming reference unless generation repeatedly loses the square mold and water-bath relationship.

missingQuestions.question: Are the dumpling wrappers round or square?
missingQuestions.reason: Top surface geometry and overlap pattern change; this affects the final reference if one is created.

missingQuestions.question: Should the final dish be cut into four clean squares or scooped more casually?
missingQuestions.reason: Clean square portions need stronger cutaway scale locks; casual scooping can lean more into juicy texture.

missingQuestions.question: Is the 20 cm mold glass, ceramic, or metal?
missingQuestions.reason: Material affects steam-bath readability and reflection; not critical if the existing baking dish asset is accepted.

clarifyingQuestions.question: Are the dumpling wrappers round or square?
clarifyingQuestions.reason: This materially affects top-surface geometry and any generated final cutaway reference.

clarifyingQuestions.question: Should the final dish be cut into four clean squares or scooped more casually?
clarifyingQuestions.reason: This changes the hero payoff, layer visibility, and scale locks.

clarifyingQuestions.question: Is the 20 cm mold glass, ceramic, or metal?
clarifyingQuestions.reason: This is useful for continuity but not blocking if the existing baking dish asset is used.
